adjoint_note: NOTE [Proven]: adjoint differentiation's O(1)-sweep amortization exists ONLY on classical simulators — there is no hardware analogue of reverse-mode backpropagation. On hardware, gradient cost remains O(d/eps^2) circuit executions per step (research doc 01-qml-accelerator.md §3.1; workflow Stage 3 step 3).
barren_plateau_constraint: quantum block: 6 qubits (<= 16), depth 2 = O(log n), local single-qubit observables — research doc §3.3-3.4 [Proven] (McClean et al.; Cerezo et al.)
execution_scaling_note: Expected executions per sample in shift mode = 1 + 2*(d_q + n) = 37 (d_q = 12 circuit weights + 6 trainable embedding angles); per step = 4736 across 128 per-sample circuits (per-sample, not broadcast: pennylane#4462; also the hardware-faithful model) — the O(d) economics of research doc §3.1 [Proven]; at hardware precision eps=1e-2 each expectation additionally needs ~10,000 shots.

hybrid.label: hybrid_vqc
hybrid.wall_clock_caveat: Simulator wall-clock validates correctness only; it is never evidence about quantum hardware speed (workflow ground rule).

control.label: classical_control
control.wall_clock_caveat: Simulator wall-clock validates correctness only; it is never evidence about quantum hardware speed (workflow ground rule).

comparison.expected_result: Null result expected: hybrid matches, not beats, the matched-parameter classical control (research doc §5 item (ii) — valuable either way) [Theoretical] experiment design.
comparison.claim_policy: No hybrid-superiority claim is made or logged unless it exceeds the control by > 2 sigma across >= 5 seeds (workflow Stage 3 acceptance); this single-seed run cannot and does not make one.
